preamble: The vocalization of the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] into the open-mid back unrounded vowel [ʌ] is a documented phonological process in Germanic dialects, where the uvular rhotic weakening produces a central or back-mid vowel resonance.

examples.note: Proposed as a derivational rule in some phonological models of German R-vocalization (e.g., T.A. Hall, 1993).
Influence of the 'Northumbrian Burr' on following vowels, sometimes resulting in mergers into the [ʌ] space.

sources: Hall, T. A. (1993). The phonology of German /r/.
